title: Public WiFi Safety
drDigitalIntro: Free public WiFi at coffee shops, airports, or hotels is convenient but risky. Other people on the same network can potentially see what you're doing. Avoid logging into your bank or entering passwords on public WiFi. If you must use public WiFi, stick to browsing sites (news, weather) and avoid anything sensitive. Consider using your phone's mobile hotspot instead — that's private and safe.

playgroundTask.question: You're at a coffee shop and need to check your bank balance. What's the safest option?

options.text: Use the free coffee shop WiFi — it's probably fine
Use your phone's mobile hotspot instead of the public WiFi
Use the coffee shop WiFi but type very quietly
Ask the barista for the WiFi password — the private one is safer

drDigitalSuccess: Your phone's cellular connection (hotspot) is private and encrypted — other people at the coffee shop can't see your banking traffic.
drDigitalHint: Public WiFi is shared with strangers. What connection is only yours?
